channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Home Affairs reports over 109,000 arrests and deportations of undocumented foreigners between 2021 and 2026
item.description: Home affairs minister Leon Schreiber has revealed that 109,735 undocumented foreign nationals were arrested and deported in South Africa over the past five financial years, spanning 2021/2022 to 2025/...

item.title: Editorial cartoon highlights for 25 May 2026
item.description: This article presents a curated selection of editorial cartoons published around 25 May 2026. Rather than reporting on a single news event, the piece functions as a visual roundup of political and soc...

item.title: Arsenal Premier League trophy presentation to be delayed after Crystal Palace match due to post-match ceremonies
item.description: Arsenal’s long-awaited Premier League title celebration will not happen immediately after their final match against Crystal Palace, as the club will have to wait around 30 minutes before officially re...

item.title: Hawks arrest Gauteng woman linked to Eastern Cape human trafficking investigation
item.description: A 30-year-old woman has been arrested by the Hawks in Johannesburg in connection with serious allegations of human trafficking and commercial sexual exploitation linked to a property in the Eastern Ca...

item.title: Experts report rising hip damage and increased hip replacement surgeries among young Indians after Covid-19
item.description: Medical experts in India have reported a significant rise in hip-related complications and surgical interventions among younger and middle-aged adults in the post-Covid period. At the 2nd DELHI HIP 36...

item.title: Two tourists shot dead in Kruger National Park as authorities launch investigation
item.description: Two foreign tourists have been reported killed in a shooting incident at South Africa’s famous Kruger National Park, according to local authorities. Police say the victims died during a violent confro...

item.title: Portraits and Career Highlights of Denzel Washington During the 1980s
item.description: The article presents a retrospective look at actor Denzel Washington during the 1980s, combining biographical information with highlights from his early acting career. It begins by outlining Washingto...

item.title: NDLEA intercepts major drug shipments, arrests foreign suspect at Lagos airport and seizes opioids worth billions
item.description: The National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA) carried out multiple anti-drug operations across Nigeria, leading to the arrest of a 63-year-old Chinese woman, Ting Hung Kiong, at the Murtala Muhamme...

item.title: How to sleep better during a heatwave as temperatures peak in the UK
item.description: The article discusses how rising temperatures across the United Kingdom are affecting people’s ability to sleep comfortably during an intense spell of warm weather. As forecasters warn of exceptional ...

item.title: Three people hospitalized following vehicle crash in Scarborough
item.description: A vehicle collision in Scarborough, Toronto, left three people hospitalized on May 24, 2026. According to the report published by CTV News Toronto, emergency crews responded to the crash early Sunday ...

item.title: More than 2,000 families displaced after massive fire hits Parola Compound in Manila
item.description: More than 2,000 families were affected after a huge fire struck the Parola Compound in San Nicolas, Binondo, Manila on May 23, 2026. Based on the Manila local government’s spot report released on Sund...

item.title: Historic 200-Year-Old Nail-Free Wooden Bridge in Dagestan Showcases Traditional Engineering
item.description: The article highlights a remarkable wooden bridge located near the village of Gulli (Juli) in the Tabasaran region of Dagestan, Russia. Estimated to be around 200 years old, the structure stands appro...

item.title: Video game developers expand unionization efforts, citing remote work policy changes and workplace conditions
item.description: A growing number of video game developers working on major franchises have joined labor unions under the Communication Workers of America (CWA), reflecting an ongoing shift in the industry toward coll...

item.title: Man interrupts President Ruto’s speech at Kilifi ceremony, briefly restrained by security
item.description: President William Ruto experienced a tense moment during a thanksgiving ceremony held for Principal Secretary Fikirini Jacobs in Ganze, Kilifi County, when a member of the public suddenly approached h...

item.title: California’s 2026 primaries shaped by voter frustration, party infighting, and political uncertainty
item.description: California’s upcoming June 2 primary elections are unfolding in an atmosphere of widespread political uncertainty and voter frustration, according to political analysts and campaign observers. The rac...

item.title: India Highlights Visa Challenges in Talks with US Secretary Rubio While Stressing Strategic Convergence
item.description: In a notable development, India has expressed its concerns regarding the United States' strict visa policies during the visit of US Secretary of State Marco Rubio. While maintaining a generally positi...

item.title: US, Iran and Regional Leaders Continue Talks on Ceasefire and Hormuz Reopening
item.description: The United States and Iran don continue discussions to finalise an agreement meant to formally end the Middle East conflict that started after US-Israeli strikes on Iran earlier this year. US Presiden...

item.title: CDN Vulnerability 'Underminr' Enables Hidden Malicious Traffic via Trusted Domains
item.description: Security researchers have disclosed a serious vulnerability in shared content delivery network (CDN) infrastructure dubbed “Underminr,” which allows attackers to conceal malicious communications behin...

item.title: Study highlights online safety risks and harmful digital exposure affecting children
item.description: A recent study has raised serious concerns about the growing risks children face while using the internet. The research does not focus on physical toxins as the headline suggests, but instead examines...

item.title: Supreme Court says no decision yet on Duterte, Dela Rosa main petition; clarifies TRO opinions to be released
item.description: The Supreme Court of the Philippines has clarified that it has not yet issued any ruling on the main petition filed by former President Rodrigo Roa Duterte and Senator Ronald “Bato” dela Rosa. This cl...
